requirements.task_name: Save the cheapest seated ticket for a Berlin concert in September 2025
requirements.task_description: Find a Berlin show in September 2025 and save the cheapest available seated ticket.

requirements.task_name: Create a 4-track playlist of acoustic songs released between 2018 and 2022
requirements.task_description: Build a playlist named 'Acoustic 2018-22' containing exactly 4 acoustic tracks released 2018–2022, in newest-to-oldest order.

requirements.task_name: Add a T-shirt and hoodie to cart with 4+ star ratings and a combined total of $80 or less
requirements.task_description: Have exactly 1 T-shirt and 1 hoodie (both size M, rating ≥ 4) in the cart with combined price ≤ $80.

requirements.task_name: Subscribe to the artist newsletter with weekly updates and 3 specific interests
requirements.task_description: Submit the newsletter form with fan@example.com, weekly frequency, and at least three interests, accepting terms.

requirements.task_name: Favorite the better album by track count under 60 minutes from 2015–2023
requirements.task_description: Compare two studio albums (2015–2023) and favorite the one with more tracks and total duration < 60 minutes (with a tie-breaker on recency).

required_variables.set_condition: Whenever searchAlbums is called with album_type === 'studio', year_from === 2015, and year_to === 2023 (the correct range for this task).
required_variables.value_to_set: An object like { album_type, year_from, year_to, sort_order, recorded_at: this._getNowIso() } serialized to JSON.
required_variables.reason: Capture that the user viewed a studio-album list filtered to 2015–2023, allowing the evaluator to confirm they used an appropriate filter context for the comparison.

required_variables.set_condition: After an album is successfully loaded in getAlbumDetail, if album.album_type === 'studio' and album.release_year is between 2015 and 2023 (inclusive).
required_variables.value_to_set: Read the current array from localStorage (default []), append album.id if not present, and write back as JSON. Optionally keep it small (e.g., first 2–3 IDs).
required_variables.reason: Record which studio albums in the 2015–2023 range the user actually opened for detail view. The evaluator can then check that among these compared albums exactly one has is_favorite === true and that this chosen album satisfies the 'more tracks under 60 minutes, tie-break on recency' rule relative to the others.

requirements.task_name: Add a post-2023 live video over 5 minutes with 10,000+ views to Watch Later
requirements.task_description: Add exactly one qualifying live video (recorded after 2023-01-01, ≥ 5 minutes, ≥ 10,000 views) to the Watch Later list.

requirements.task_name: Plan an October 2025 mini-tour with 2 shows in different countries using General Admission tickets
requirements.task_description: Add two October 2025 shows in different countries with General Admission tickets to an itinerary.

requirements.task_name: Favorite a 2020 album song with 'midnight' in the title and view its lyrics in Spanish
requirements.task_description: Locate a 2020-album track with 'midnight' in the title, switch its lyrics to Spanish, and favorite the track.

required_variables.set_condition: Every time getTrackLyrics(trackId, language) successfully returns (i.e., after the lookup completes), regardless of language; this will capture when Spanish ('es' or the site's Spanish code) is requested.
required_variables.value_to_set: An object like { track_id: trackId, language: language, viewed_at: this._getNowIso() } serialized to JSON.
required_variables.reason: Record which track's lyrics were last requested and in which language. The evaluator can then verify that there exists a track that (1) is favorited (tracks[].is_favorite), (2) belongs to a 2020 album and has 'midnight' in its title, and (3) matches task8_lyricsSelection.track_id with task8_lyricsSelection.language equal to the Spanish language code, demonstrating that Spanish lyrics were actively viewed for that track.
